exercises.title: Race condition — expected vs actual
exercises.scenario: counter++ is not atomic — threads interleave and lose updates.
exercises.expected_solution: Less than 500 — typically between 300-499. The exact value varies each run due to the race condition on c++.
hints: c++ is READ-ADD-WRITE — three steps
Two threads can both READ the same value, both ADD 1, and both WRITE the same incremented value
Result: one increment is lost

exercises.title: Add synchronized to fix race condition
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blank to make increment() thread-safe.

blanks.hint: Keyword that acquires the object lock
Also needed for reads of shared state

hints: Both read and write operations need synchronization
synchronized before return type: public synchronized int getCount()

exercises.title: Demonstrate and fix the race condition
exercises.scenario: Show incorrect result without sync, correct result with sync.
exercises.instructions:
Run the counter increment with 5 threads × 200 each:
1. Unsafe version — print 'Unsafe: N' (likely < 1000)
2. Safe version (synchronized) — print 'Safe: 1000'

Expected output:
Unsafe: [some number < 1000]
Safe: 1000
exercises.starter_code: // Show race condition then fix it
hints: Run both versions and compare
Unsafe result is non-deterministic but usually < 1000
Safe result is always exactly 1000

exercises.title: Thread-safe bank account
exercises.scenario: Synchronized methods protect all account operations.
exercises.instructions:
Create `BankAccount` with synchronized deposit(double) and withdraw(double). Test with 10 threads: 5 deposit ₹200, 5 withdraw ₹100. Start balance ₹1000.

Expected output:
Final balance: ₹1500.00
hints: synchronized on deposit(), withdraw(), getBalance()

exercises.title: volatile flag for thread stop
exercises.scenario: volatile boolean signals thread to stop.
exercises.instructions:
Create a thread that prints 'Working...' every 200ms until a volatile flag is set. Main sets the flag after 600ms. Thread should print 2-3 times then stop.

Expected output:
Working...
Working...
Working...
Stopped
hints: static volatile boolean stop = false
Worker checks while (!stop)
Main sets stop = true after 650ms

exercises.title: synchronized block — minimize critical section
exercises.scenario: Synchronized block on specific object — not whole method.
exercises.instructions:
BankAccount with synchronized BLOCK (not whole method). balance field protected by a private lock object. Simulate 200ms pre-processing before the critical section.

Expected output (after concurrent test):
Final: ₹1000.00
hints: private final Object lock = new Object() — dedicated lock
synchronized (lock) { ... } — only the critical section
Five deposits + five withdrawals of equal amount → same final balance

exercises.title: Thread-safe counter class
exercises.scenario: Complete synchronized counter with multiple operations.
exercises.instructions:
Build `ThreadSafeCounter` with: synchronized increment(), decrement(), reset(), getCount(). Verify with 20 threads: 10 increment 50 times, 10 decrement 30 times. Start at 0.

Expected output:
Final count: 200
hints: 10×50 increments = 500; 10×30 decrements = 300; net = 200
All four methods must be synchronized

exercises.title: volatile vs synchronized — know the difference
exercises.scenario: volatile fixes visibility; synchronized fixes both.
exercises.instructions:
Demonstrate that volatile fixes the stop-flag visibility problem but synchronized is needed for a counter. Print:
- volatile flag: WORKS (thread sees stop)
- volatile counter: MAY BE WRONG (race condition)
- synchronized counter: ALWAYS CORRECT

Expected output:
Volatile flag worked: true
Synchronized counter: 1000
exercises.starter_code: // volatile for flag, synchronized for counter

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Volatile flag worked: true
Synchronized counter: 1000

hints: Volatile flag: thread sees the updated value immediately
volatile int counter: still races on counter++ — not shown in expected output
synchronized block: always exactly 1000

exercises.title: Trace synchronized method — which threads block?
exercises.scenario: synchronized prevents concurrent execution of protected methods.
exercises.expected_solution: A and B always appear together (no X/Y between them). X and Y always appear together. Possible outputs: A B X Y or X Y A B or A B X Y — but NEVER A X B Y or X A Y B because synchronized prevents interleaving within the method.

test_cases.expected: A B stay adjacent; X Y stay adjacent; order between pairs varies

hints: Each print() call is atomic — no other thread can enter while one is inside
A then B from t1, X then Y from t2 — but pair order may vary
Can't get A X B Y — once A starts, B follows immediately (no interleaving)

exercises.title: Deadlock demonstration
exercises.instructions:
Demonstrate a potential deadlock: Thread A acquires lock1 then wants lock2. Thread B acquires lock2 then wants lock1. Use join(1000) with timeout to detect if deadlock occurred.

Expected output (may detect deadlock):
Starting deadlock demo...
[either completes or detects deadlock within 1s]
exercises.starter_code: // Deadlock demo — consistent lock ordering is the fix
hints: Deadlock: T1 holds lock1, waits for lock2. T2 holds lock2, waits for lock1.
join(1000) detects deadlock: isAlive() == true after timeout
Fix: both threads acquire lock1 then lock2 (same order)

exercises.title: Thread-safe inventory manager
exercises.instructions:
Build `InventoryManager` with synchronized `purchase(String item, int qty)` returning boolean. Multiple threads try to buy the last 10 units of 'USB Hub'. Only exactly 10 purchases of qty=1 should succeed.

Expected output:
Successful purchases: 10
Failed purchases: [rest]
Final stock: 0

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Successful purchases: 10
Failed purchases: 10
Final stock: 0

hints: synchronized purchase() — check-then-decrement is atomic
Use AtomicInteger for counting results (thread-safe counter)
20 threads, 10 units: exactly 10 succeed, 10 fail

exercises.title: Synchronized singleton
exercises.instructions:
Implement a thread-safe Singleton with lazy initialization. Use double-checked locking pattern with volatile + synchronized.

Expected output:
Singleton instance 1 == Singleton instance 2: true
Only one instance created: true

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Singleton instance 1 == Singleton instance 2: true
Only one instance created: true

hints: volatile: ensures constructor completion is visible before reference is published
Double-check: first null check avoids lock on every call; second null check inside lock prevents race
synchronized(Singleton.class) — class-level lock for static context

exercises.title: Bounded blocking queue — producer-consumer
exercises.scenario: Synchronized data structure for producer-consumer pattern.
exercises.instructions:
Build a simple `BoundedQueue<T>` (capacity 5) with:
- synchronized put(T) — blocks if full (use wait/notifyAll)
- synchronized take() — blocks if empty

Test with 2 producers (each putting 5 items) and 2 consumers (each taking 5 items).

Expected output (order varies):
[10 put messages]
[10 take messages]
Done
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// BoundedQueue with wait/notifyAll — producer-consumer
hints: while (condition) wait() — must use while, not if (spurious wakeups)
notifyAll() after put/take wakes all waiting threads
synchronized on both put() and take() — same object lock

exercises.title: Concurrent order processing — race condition audit
exercises.scenario: Identify and fix all race conditions in an order processor.
exercises.starter_code: // Fix the race conditions in OrderProcessor
hints: Both orderCount++ and totalRevenue += must be synchronized together
One synchronized method protects both updates atomically
Getter methods also need synchronization for visibility

exercises.title: Thread-safe LRU cache — synchronized with eviction
exercises.scenario: Synchronized cache with LRU eviction under concurrent access.
exercises.instructions:
Build `ThreadSafeCache<K, V>` (max 3 entries, LRU eviction) with synchronized get(K) and put(K, V). Use LinkedHashMap for LRU. Test with concurrent puts and gets — verify no corruption.

Expected output:
After concurrent ops, cache size <= 3: true
Cache operations completed without exception: true
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// ThreadSafeCache with LRU eviction

test_cases.expected_stdout:
After concurrent ops, cache size <= 3: true
Cache operations completed without exception: true

hints: LinkedHashMap(16, 0.75f, true) — accessOrder=true for LRU
Override removeEldestEntry for auto-eviction
All public methods synchronized — LinkedHashMap is NOT thread-safe
